title: Taiwan MND reports 29 PLA aircraft detected with 15 crossing median line in joint patrol
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 29 PLA aircraft including 15 that crossed the median line entering northern, central, and southwestern ADIZ, plus 6 PLAN vessels and 2 official ships conducting 'joint combat readiness patrols.'
contents:
## Taiwan Reports Elevated PLA Activity Around Island

### Daily Activity Report
Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense reported detecting 29 PLA aircraft, 6 PLAN vessels, and 2 official ships operating around Taiwan in the 24-hour period ending 6 a.m. local time on May 2. Of the 29 aircraft detected, 15 crossed the Taiwan Strait median line and its extensions, entering Taiwan's northern, central, and southwestern air defense identification zones.

### Aircraft Composition
The MND detailed that various types of fighter aircraft, support aircraft, and unmanned aerial vehicles participated in the operation. The PLA aircraft began appearing from 8:01 a.m. on May 1, with the ministry identifying J-10, J-16, and KJ-500 early warning aircraft among the platforms detected.

### PLA Framing
China's military characterized the activity as "joint combat readiness patrols," a designation Taiwan's MND described as a pretext for harassing airspace and waters surrounding the island. The ROC Armed Forces deployed mission aircraft, naval vessels, and coastal-based missile systems to monitor and respond.

### Monthly Trend
The elevated sortie count continues the pattern of sustained gray zone pressure that the PRC has maintained since September 2020, with April 2026 recording approximately 187 aircraft contacts and 207 ship contacts around Taiwan.

**Source:** Taiwan MND official X post, CNA (Central News Agency)
